response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level pedigree, hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at Google, and demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience from Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s). However, Python is a stated must-have and is absent from the skills section and every role description — a hard gap for this position. The current Google role is also very new (~1 month), making it difficult to assess depth of AI-native environment experience. The overall profile is competitive enough to warrant a screen call specifically to probe Python proficiency and distributed-systems depth.
strengths: Currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at Google, directly matching the AI-native engineering environment requirement
Dunnhumby role demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems work at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O)
Consistent Staff-level IC track record across multiple companies with evidence of technical strategy, open-source platform ownership, and community knowledge sharing
concerns: Python is completely absent from the skills list and all role descriptions despite being a must-have for this position
Current Google role started April 2025 (~1 month tenure), providing insufficient signal on depth of contribution in the AI-agent space
Cross-functional stakeholder management and agile ceremony ownership are not surfaced in any role description, leaving a gap against the non-technical must-haves
